summary:
Root calcification typically finishes at about 6 years old; this applies generally unless specific abnormalities are present which can cause deviations from normal timelines. The answer choice reflecting a close approximation would likely correspond well here as per standard development milestones.
Tooth root mineralization usually concludes around six years when considering average cases without unusual conditions affecting it differently. This timeframe matches closely enough what was found across multiple references provided during analysis.
